conbody.p: This section describes the new features in the Juniper Cloud-Native Router (JCNR) 24.4 release.

section.title: New Features in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 24.4

li.p: Support for Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternates (TI-LFA) with fast reroute (FRR)—JCNR supports implementing Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternates (TI-LFA) with fast reroute (FRR) in SR-MPLS. TI-LFA is supported on IS-IS implementations for SR-MPLS. It is also supported on OSPF implementations as a technology preview feature. Only protection against link failure is supported with one primary and one secondary (backup) path.

Support for Layer-3 Class of Service (CoS)—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports Layer 3 Class of Service (CoS), also known as L3 Quality of Service (QoS). JCNR supports the following classifiers, policers and rewrite/marker implementations:
Single-rate three-color marker (srTCM) and Two-rate three-color marker (trTCM) policers
Rewrite rules supports copying outer IPv4/IPv6 DSCP marking to inner IP header DSCP field as well as MPLS EXP bit marking

Additional support for L3/L4 Access Lists—JCNR now supports MPLS protocol family for L3/L4 access lists. It also supports additional match nonterminating actions including syslog, routing-instance, forwarding class and policer.
Additional Telemetry Support—JCNR now supports telemetry for interfaces extended statistics, firewall filter counters and TI-LFA statistics via Prometheus and gNMI.

Support for Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP)—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports LLDP on Layer-3 interfaces to advertise capabilities, identity, and other information onto a LAN. LLDP is supported on Layer 3 interfaces.

Support for DHCP Relay—Juniper Cloud-Native Router can be configured as a Stateless DHCP Relay agent for an L2-L3 deployment that can relay DHCP messages between cascaded Next-Generation Distributed Units (NGDUs) and an external DHCP server. It supports simple packet forwarding non-snooping DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 relay feature between the DHCP client and DHCP server.

Support for Layer 3 CoS scheduler—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports L3 class of service (CoS) scheduler implemention to compute the priority of packets. It implements a strict priority 8-queue scheduler, with priority order high to low. The forwarding class is directly mapped to scheduler priority.

ul.li: Support for cSRX Telemetry—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports telemetry capabilities for cSRX deployed as a service chain for IPSec security services. Visibility via Prometheus-based API and gRPC Network Management Interface (gNMI) is supported.

li.p: Support for vRouter timestamping TWAMP test packets—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports vRouter timestamping for Two-Way Active Management Protocol (TWAMP) test packets in light mode implementation. The vRouter uses hardware timestamping if the underlying NIC supports it, else it defaults to the kernel system clock.
Support for Loop Detection in Pure L2 Mode—Juniper Cloud-Native Router implements Layer 2 loop detection mechanisms in the vRouter by detecting frequent MAC address movements between ports.

ul.li: Support for Host Protection using Control Plane Policing—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports host protection against DDoS attacks. You can configure rate-limiting for host traffic, based on protocol classification on the loopback interface lo0.0.

li.p: Support for Ubuntu OS—Juniper Cloud-Native Router is now supported on Ubuntu for bare metal, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP) deployments.

Support for preflight and postflight checks—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports preflight and postflight checks under the Juniper Cloud-Native Router Readiness framework. Preflight checks verify that your cluster nodes can support JCNR and postflight checks verify that your Cloud-Native Router installation is working properly.
Support for local persistence of cRPD configuration—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports local persistence of cRPD configuration across reboots, pod restarts, and upgrades.

Support for Service Module Host-Based Routing—Juniper Cloud-Native Router Service Module for Host-Based Routing augments the Operator Service Module framework to provide a cRPD host-based routing solution for your Kubernetes cluster. You can now leverage an external cRPD installation within the host's network namespace to provide a more feature-rich networking implementation for your pod network.

Support for Validation Factory—Juniper Cloud-Native Router Validation Factory is a library of test profiles that you can run to validate your Cloud-Native Router deployment. Implemented as a Helm chart, Validation Factory makes it easy for you to test your Cloud-Native Router deployment for evaluation or even prior to going live in a production environment. Validation Factory is initially supported for Layer 3 tests in Wind River deployments only.
